node.text:
Trees are among the most cost-effective, nature-based solutions to help combat the climate crisis. Every tree helps in: 1) Air pollution reduction, 2) carbon sequestration, & 3) significant health benefits—but, there’s often inequality in where trees get planted in many cities. 🌱🏙🌳 

@nature_org teams and @CityPlantsLA use cloud technology to grow a greener future by engaging Angelenos to plant & care for trees. See how #AWS customers use the cloud to fight climate change in our season finale of Climate Next. Link in bio. 🔗 #ClimateChange

node.text: Learn how to level-up your skills and career through the journey of @keshawillz, an AWS Machine Learning Hero. Kesha transformed her engineering skills from Java developer to ML practitioner by learning hands-on with AWS AI solutions like AWS DeepLens, Amazon Rekognition, and Amazon SageMaker; as well as TensorFlow, Jupyter, and Python. As an ML practitioner, she integrates technology into everyday life by developing ML models using computer vision on SageMaker to help us better connect with people around us. Hear the challenges she faced on her journey and how she overcame them using AWS AI solutions.

node.title: From Java Developer to Machine Learning Practitioner in 6 Months

node.text:
If love were measured in AWS fiber-optic cable, you’d be able to go to the Moon and back not just once, but 11 times. 

That’s over nine million kilometers of terrestrial and subsea fiber-optic cabling connecting people worldwide through our global network.

We hope you find someone (or already have) who loves you to the Moon and back — or at least as much as AWS optical engineers love fiber.

node.text:
Nerding out over how cool this is

Every @NFL snap generates millions of data points. Twenty-two players accelerating, colliding, and changing direction in fractions of a second.

For a decade, @nextgenstats powered by @amazonwebservices has turned that chaos into data that forms the backbone of how teams scout, strategize, and win—and how the league studies officiating, player safety, and rule changes.

Seventy-five machine learning models. Insights in under one second. Here's how it works.
